title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage encryption is done with  customer managed keys

input_row.title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage encryption is done with  customer managed keys
input_row.description: Users can store objects in IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets by providing their own encryption keys which get applied at a per object level.
input_row.rationale: Users can have added security and granular control over the encryption keys at a per object level.
input_row.audit: Using Console: N/A Using API/CLI: Use of Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys (SSE-C) can be validated by the following steps: Note: Ensure that you have completed the configuration setup to use the CLI by following the guidelines on the Using the AWS CLI . Review the metadata of the object that is encrypted using the customer-provided key. The operation can be performed using an API call or via a command-line interface. Here is an example call to get the object metadata: aws --endpoint https://s3.private.au-syd.cloud-object- storage.appdomain.cloud s3api head-object --bucket --key --sse-customer-algorithm=AES256 --sse-customer- key= 2. The presence of the object headers SSECustomerKeyMD5 and SSECustomerAlgorithm from the API/CLI response should confirm that the object is encrypted using the key.
input_row.remediation: Using Console: N/A Using API/CLI: Objects can be uploaded with your own key by using a PUT object request with key specific headers. Please refer to Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys for additional information.
input_row.impact: Users can configure Cloud Object Storage and use their own root keys when uploading objects. For any key rotation (or new key usage) users will have to issue a GET operation with the old key and a PUT operation with the new key.
input_row.references: 1. • Data encryption with IBM Cloud Object Storage: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- encryption 2. • Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage-sse- c
input_row.default_value: By default, all objects stored in IBM Cloud Object Storage are encrypted by using randomly generated keys and an all-or-nothing-transform (AONT) also known as provider managed keys. Clients can provide their own encryption keys for a per-object level encryption by using the IBM Cloud Object Storage Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys (SSE-C) option.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_with_customer_managed_keys_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use IBM Cloud CLI commands to interact with the IBM Cloud Object Storage service to validate the configuration of encryption at the object level using customer-managed keys.
- Specific commands to use:
  - `ibmcloud cos head-object --bucket my-bucket --key my-object --sse-customer-algorithm=AES256 --sse-customer-key=my-key` to retrieve the object metadata and check for the presence of the appropriate headers.
- Resources to enumerate: All objects within specified IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets.
- Validate that the following headers are present in the response:
  - `SSECustomerKeyMD5`: Indicates that the object is encrypted with a customer-managed key.
  - `SSECustomerAlgorithm`: Should be set to `AES256` confirming the algorithm used for encryption.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** Both `SSECustomerKeyMD5` and `SSECustomerAlgorithm` headers are present and correctly configured.
  - **Fail:** One or both headers are missing or not configured as per expectations.
- Documentation for further reference:
  - [Data encryption with IBM Cloud Object Storage](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage-encryption)
  - [Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage-sse-c)
